standards: PRISMA 2020 (Page et al. 2021, BMJ 372:n71): Identification, Screening, Included
PRISMA-trAIce R1 (Holst et al. 2025, JMIR AI 4:e80247, proposed extension): flow distinguishes AI decisions from human decisions
method: Every count in this file was recomputed on 2026-06-09 by an agent running anchored regular-expression counts (ripgrep) over the raw CSVs in benchmark/data/, which are CRLF files; patterns that anchor at line end use \r?$. Each count carries a source object naming the file and the counting rule. Counts that could not be recomputed from benchmark/data/ are taken from knowledge/conformance-audit.md or knowledge/verification-empirical-core.md and say so in their source. The scripted, committed replay (Stage R2 of knowledge/plan.md) that supersedes this agent recount is pending; until it exists, the evidence flag above applies to every number here.
consistency_checks: papers_full.csv: the four (Is_Duplicate, Has_HA) flag combinations count 200 + 92 + 31 + 3 = 326, equal to the row-marker count, so every data row matched exactly once.
human_assessment.csv: 142 Include + 161 Exclude = 303, equal to the row-marker count.
llm_assessment_10k.csv: 232 Include + 94 Exclude = 326, equal to the row-marker count.

merged_comparison.csv: 338 rows total; the four confusion cells sum to 291; 338 - 326 = 12 human-only and 338 - 303 = 35 AI-only, matching benchmark/results/agreement_metrics.json metadata (291 / 12 / 35).

Exclusion reasons sum to 160 of 161 Exclude rows; the residual row is not attributable by per-line pattern matching (multiline quoted field), the same remainder knowledge/conformance-audit.md records.

source.how: data rows counted with the row-start regex ^\d+,[A-Z0-9]{8}, (sequential ID, then 8-character Zotero key); agent recount 2026-06-09

claimed_source_tool_distribution.scope: only the 303 records of the human assessment track carry a Source_Tool value; the column predates the repository record and is not auditable (knowledge/conformance-audit.md, non-auditable acquisition section)

source.how: rows matching ^\d+,[A-Z0-9]{8},.*,<tool>, per tool name; the five counts sum exactly to the 303 row count; agent recount 2026-06-09, matching the CSV-parsed counts in knowledge/conformance-audit.md
taken from the audit, which counted the restored RIS files in deep-research/restored/ mapped via corpus/source_tool_mapping.json; not recomputable from benchmark/data/
lines ending ,Yes,Yes\r?$ (92) plus ,Yes,No\r?$ (3) for the trailing Is_Duplicate,Has_HA column pair; agent recount 2026-06-09

duplicates_removed_before_screening.note: deduplication was not a separate identification-phase step; it happened inside human screening as 67 exclusions with reason Duplicate (see human lane below). The flow is recorded as it happened, per knowledge/conformance-audit.md.

source.how: no removal step exists in the data; all 326 records, including the 95 flagged duplicates, enter the screening files

identification.non_auditable: Query dates per provider, provider-side model versions, the executed deep-research prompt instantiations, and the merging and metadata cleaning step are not reconstructable from the repository (knowledge/conformance-audit.md; knowledge/paper-integrity.md section 4).

source.how: data rows counted with ^\d+,[A-Z0-9]{8},; agent recount 2026-06-09
lines containing ,Include, (the decision token is the only such occurrence outside quoted prose; 142 + 161 = 303 closes against the row count); agent recount 2026-06-09
lines containing ,Exclude,; agent recount 2026-06-09

exclusion_reasons.vocabulary_note: the value Other (5) is outside the controlled vocabulary of benchmark/config/categories.yaml and the 7 empty cells violate it; documented as a hygiene finding in knowledge/conformance-audit.md

source.how: lines matching ,Exclude,<reason>, per controlled reason and ,Exclude,, for empty; per-line counts sum to 160 of 161, the residual row sits in a multiline quoted field and is not pattern-attributable, matching the remainder in knowledge/conformance-audit.md; agent recount 2026-06-09
lines ending ,No,No\r?$ (31) plus ,Yes,No\r?$ (3) for Has_HA=No; agent recount 2026-06-09
lines ending ,No,Yes\r?$ (200) plus ,Yes,Yes\r?$ (92) for Has_HA=Yes; agent recount 2026-06-09
derived: 338 merged rows (counted with ^[A-Z0-9]{8},) minus the 326 AI-track rows; matches papers_human_only in benchmark/results/agreement_metrics.json; agent recount 2026-06-09

human_lane.per_record_reviewer_identity: absent; the CSV has no reviewer column. The track is one consolidated annotation by a primary assessor (R1) with corrections or additions by a second assessor (R2) that are not traceable per record; no inter-human reliability statistic exists (knowledge/conformance-audit.md; knowledge/verification-empirical-core.md).

ai_lane.role: advisory sibling assessment, stored separately from the human track and never merged destructively

source.how: taken from the audit, which cites the pinned snapshot id in benchmark/scripts/run_llm_assessment.py; max_tokens 1024 pinned, temperature and top-p unrecorded API defaults

ai_lane.input: title plus abstract per paper from papers_full.csv (knowledge document conditions belong to the separate 2x2 experiment, not to this round-1 flow)

taken from the verification document, which recomputed it by hand from the verified cell counts; also reported in benchmark/results/agreement_metrics.json. Interpretation constraints (PABAK 0.0241, kappa-max 0.5081, content-only sensitivity) live in that document and bind any use of this number.

paired.open_discrepancy: papers_full.csv flags 292 records as humanly assessed while only 291 pair in the benchmark merge; which record fails to pair cannot be determined without re-running the merge (knowledge/conformance-audit.md). Recorded as an open inconsistency, not smoothed over.

source.how: identical to the human lane Include count above; the human decision is the binding record
derived: both_include (100) plus human_include_ai_exclude (34); the remaining 8 human includes sit among the 12 human-only records outside the AI corpus file (marginal closure: 134 + 8 = 142)
identical to the AI lane advisory_include count above; advisory only, never binding
identical to the both_include confusion cell above

reports_and_text_basis.note: PRISMA 2020 asks for reports sought and retrieved. In this round, human screening decisions were not conditioned on retrieval; decisions exist for papers whose text was never acquired. The chain below is the documented acquisition and serving state, not a screening gate.

named_gaps: No pre-registered protocol and no pre-specified AI use exist (PRISMA 2020 item 24, trAIce M1); non-reconstructable by definition.
34 corpus papers carry no human decision; the AI lane covers all 326, so for these 34 only an advisory assessment exists.
Acquisition provenance is non-auditable beyond 34 RIS-corroborated records; the executed deep-research prompts were never committed.
One-record discrepancy between papers_full.csv (292 flagged humanly assessed) and the benchmark pairing (291).
No per-record reviewer identity inside the human lane (assessors R1 and R2 named only collectively).
Exclusion-reason vocabulary was documented but not enforced: 5 Other, 7 empty cells.
